item.title: Bullish JP Morgan bumps AAPL price target to $345
item.description: JP Morgan increased the price target for Apple's stock to $345, insisting that the RAM-driven hardware cost increases won't impact long-term revenue gains.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68179-143719-67551-142284-67319-141682-macstudio1-xl-xl-xl.jpg" alt="Compact silver desktop computer on a desk beneath a monitor, with front ports visible and a small indicator light, set against a softly lit modern background with red and blue accents" height="720"><br><span>Sales will continue fine for premium products like the Mac Studio, JP Morgan reckons. </span></div><br>Late in June, Apple finally gave in and <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/25/apple-confirms-price-nikes-across-macs-ipads-and-more">raised the price</a> of many products, in the face of the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/17/ai-driven-ship-shortages-to-cause-apple-product-price-increase-says-cook">global memory crisis</a>. In the view of JP Morgan, it's not that big an issue for the company.<br><br>In a note to investors seen by <em>AppleInsider</em> on Tuesday, JP Morgan has increased its price target for Apple to $345. This is an increase of $20 <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/01/30/jp-morgan-hikes-apple-stock-target-to-325-after-record-breaking-earnings">from January</a>, when it last raised the stock target price to $325.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/07/bullish-jp-morgan-bumps-aapl-price-target-to-345?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244890?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: JP Morgan increased the price target for Apple's stock to $345, insisting that the RAM-driven hardware cost increases won't impact long-term revenue gains.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68179-143719-67551-142284-67319-141682-macstudio1-xl-xl-xl.jpg" alt="Compact silver desktop computer on a desk beneath a monitor, with front ports visible and a small indicator light, set against a softly lit modern background with red and blue accents" height="720"><br><span>Sales will continue fine for premium products like the Mac Studio, JP Morgan reckons. </span></div><br>Late in June, Apple finally gave in and <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/25/apple-confirms-price-nikes-across-macs-ipads-and-more">raised the price</a> of many products, in the face of the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/17/ai-driven-ship-shortages-to-cause-apple-product-price-increase-says-cook">global memory crisis</a>. In the view of JP Morgan, it's not that big an issue for the company.<br><br>In a note to investors seen by <em>AppleInsider</em> on Tuesday, JP Morgan has increased its price target for Apple to $345. This is an increase of $20 <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/01/30/jp-morgan-hikes-apple-stock-target-to-325-after-record-breaking-earnings">from January</a>, when it last raised the stock target price to $325.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/07/bullish-jp-morgan-bumps-aapl-price-target-to-345?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244890?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Terramaster F4-425 Pro review: Excellent set-and-forget networking hardware
item.description: The TerraMaster F4-425 Pro is a compact four-bay NAS that punches above its price class, with an eight-core Intel chip, three M.2 slots, and dual 5GbE networking, at the cost of a few real but manageable caveats.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68163-143679-terramasterreview-1-xl.jpg" alt="Small rectangular silver external hard drive enclosure with a black front panel featuring four vertical drive bays and a tiny blue status light, resting on a gray fabric-covered surface." height="738"><br><span>TerraMaster F4-425 Pro review</span></div><br>NAS devices have evolved well beyond simple network drives. They now run media servers, security camera systems, and containerized apps, all from one box on the home or office network.<br><br>TerraMaster pitches the <a href="https://amazon.com/TERRAMASTER-F4-425-Pro-NAS-Storage/dp/B0GTLTZPMC/?th=1&tag=apinsiderreview-20" rel="nofollow">F4-425 Pro</a> squarely at that all-in-one role. It pairs a decently powerful Intel chip with unusually flexible storage for the class and price point.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/07/terramaster-f4-425-pro-review-excellent-set-and-forget-networking-hardware?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244888?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: iPhone Air 2 battery expected to hit 30 hours of use from a single charge
item.description: The <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-air" title="iPhone Air" data-kpt="1">iPhone Air</a> 2 is rumored to last longer than the original thanks to a much larger battery, fixing one of the biggest flaws of the super-thin smartphone.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68178-143717-66415-139283-65764-137738-iPhone-Air-camera-side-xl-xl-xl.jpg" alt="Close-up of a modern white smartphone lying face down, highlighting the rear camera lens, flash, smooth metal edges, and side button on a clean light-colored surface" height="738"><br><span>The current-gen iPhone Air</span></div><br>The iPhone Air was a <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/25/10/17/i-took-an-iphone-air-on-vacation-to-test-its-two-biggest-compromises——and-came-home-surprised">device of compromise</a>, with its thin frame made possible by some size-cutting specification choices. For the second-gen model, Apple is apparently seeking to right one of the perceived wrongs.<br><br>According to Weibo leaker Digital Chat Station on <a href="https://weibo.com/6048569942/R7zsmexgd">July 7</a>, citing the supply chain, the iPhone Air 2 will have a battery with a capacity of around 3,500mAh.<br><br><br> <strong>Rumor Score:</strong> 🤔 Possible <br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/07/iphone-air-2-battery-expected-to-hit-30-hours-of-use-from-a-single-charge?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244889?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

For the second-gen model, Apple is apparently seeking to right one of the perceived wrongs.<br><br>According to Weibo leaker Digital Chat Station on <a href="https://weibo.com/6048569942/R7zsmexgd">July 7</a>, citing the supply chain, the iPhone Air 2 will have a battery with a capacity of around 3,500mAh.<br><br><br> <strong>Rumor Score:</strong> 🤔 Possible <br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/07/iphone-air-2-battery-expected-to-hit-30-hours-of-use-from-a-single-charge?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244889?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Apple Home AI features locked behind 2TB iCloud+ plan
item.description: Whether you subscribe to the 2TB iCloud+ tier individually or pay for Apple One Premier, you're getting the new Apple Intelligence in <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-home" title="Apple Home" data-kpt="1">Apple Home</a> features announced during WWDC 2026.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68175-143716-Home-Video-AI-labels-and-search-iOS-27-xl.jpg" alt="Smartphone screen showing a security camera app with noteworthy clips of Amazon and mail deliveries, plus a list of recorded motion alerts on a dark interface against a yellow background" height="738"><br><span>Apple Home AI updates are behind an iCloud+ subscription tier</span></div><br>Apple didn't break out exactly what customers might have to pay in order to access its most advanced AI features. While there aren't any separate AI subscriptions or token purchase programs, users will need to spend more cash for the most access.<br><br>In the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/macos-golden-gate" title="macOS Golden Gate" data-kpt="1">macOS Golden Gate</a> beta release notes, Apple has confirmed that the Apple Home AI features will require the 2TB <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/icloud" title="iCloud" data-kpt="1">iCloud+</a> plan. On its own, that is a $10 a month plan, or is included with the $37.95 <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-one" title="Apple One" data-kpt="1">Apple One</a> Premier subscription tier.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/apple-home-ai-features-locked-behind-2tb-icloud-plan?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244887?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: Whether you subscribe to the 2TB iCloud+ tier individually or pay for Apple One Premier, you're getting the new Apple Intelligence in <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-home" title="Apple Home" data-kpt="1">Apple Home</a> features announced during WWDC 2026.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68175-143716-Home-Video-AI-labels-and-search-iOS-27-xl.jpg" alt="Smartphone screen showing a security camera app with noteworthy clips of Amazon and mail deliveries, plus a list of recorded motion alerts on a dark interface against a yellow background" height="738"><br><span>Apple Home AI updates are behind an iCloud+ subscription tier</span></div><br>Apple didn't break out exactly what customers might have to pay in order to access its most advanced AI features. While there aren't any separate AI subscriptions or token purchase programs, users will need to spend more cash for the most access.<br><br>In the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/macos-golden-gate" title="macOS Golden Gate" data-kpt="1">macOS Golden Gate</a> beta release notes, Apple has confirmed that the Apple Home AI features will require the 2TB <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/icloud" title="iCloud" data-kpt="1">iCloud+</a> plan. On its own, that is a $10 a month plan, or is included with the $37.95 <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-one" title="Apple One" data-kpt="1">Apple One</a> Premier subscription tier.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/apple-home-ai-features-locked-behind-2tb-icloud-plan?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244887?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Apple is warning when your AI prompts go to Google's servers, just like with ChatGPT
item.description: Image generation features found in <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-creator-studio" title="Apple Creator Studio" data-kpt="1">Apple Creator Studio</a> rely on Google Cloud servers, but users will be warned before prompts are sent to the third-party AI tool.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68174-143715-Google-Cloud-request-in-Pages-image-generation-AI-xl.jpg" alt="Colorful electronic device icons on a black background with a centered popup dialog asking to send text to Google Cloud, offering Allow Once, Always Allow, and Cancel buttons" height="738"><br><span>Apple warns users before sending prompts to Google Cloud</span></div><br><a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-intelligence" title="Apple Intelligence" data-kpt="1">Apple Intelligence</a> is <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/08/apples-new-foundation-models-dont-contain-a-drop-of-gemini-as-we-said-they-wouldnt">powered by</a> Apple Foundation Models found on your <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhone</a> and in Apple's Private Cloud Compute servers. Those are distinct features and models from the integrations that utilize third-party AI tools like Google Cloud and ChatGPT.<br><br><a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/30/if-you-wanted-more-ai-in-apples-creator-studio-tuesdays-update-gives-it-to-you">After updating</a> to the latest Apple Creator Studio version, users are encountering a new pop-up, whether they are running <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ios-26" title="iOS 26" data-kpt="1">iOS 26</a> or <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ios-27" title="iOS 27" data-kpt="1">iOS 27</a>. That pop-up warns that the user's prompt will be sent to a Google Cloud server, but won't be used for training.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/apple-is-warning-when-your-ai-prompts-go-to-googles-servers-just-like-with-chatgpt?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244886?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: Image generation features found in <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-creator-studio" title="Apple Creator Studio" data-kpt="1">Apple Creator Studio</a> rely on Google Cloud servers, but users will be warned before prompts are sent to the third-party AI tool.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68174-143715-Google-Cloud-request-in-Pages-image-generation-AI-xl.jpg" alt="Colorful electronic device icons on a black background with a centered popup dialog asking to send text to Google Cloud, offering Allow Once, Always Allow, and Cancel buttons" height="738"><br><span>Apple warns users before sending prompts to Google Cloud</span></div><br><a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-intelligence" title="Apple Intelligence" data-kpt="1">Apple Intelligence</a> is <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/08/apples-new-foundation-models-dont-contain-a-drop-of-gemini-as-we-said-they-wouldnt">powered by</a> Apple Foundation Models found on your <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhone</a> and in Apple's Private Cloud Compute servers. Those are distinct features and models from the integrations that utilize third-party AI tools like Google Cloud and ChatGPT.<br><br><a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/30/if-you-wanted-more-ai-in-apples-creator-studio-tuesdays-update-gives-it-to-you">After updating</a> to the latest Apple Creator Studio version, users are encountering a new pop-up, whether they are running <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ios-26" title="iOS 26" data-kpt="1">iOS 26</a> or <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ios-27" title="iOS 27" data-kpt="1">iOS 27</a>. That pop-up warns that the user's prompt will be sent to a Google Cloud server, but won't be used for training.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/apple-is-warning-when-your-ai-prompts-go-to-googles-servers-just-like-with-chatgpt?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244886?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: watchOS 27 beta 3 brings Siri AI to the Apple Watch
item.description: Apple Watch owners can now finally use the new-and-improved Siri AI, thanks to <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/watchos-27" title="watchOS 27" data-kpt="1">watchOS 27</a> beta 3.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68173-143712-Siriwatch-Cropped-xl.jpg" alt="Apple Watch screen showing Siri request: Hey Siri, show me some good stretches for running recovery, with a searching status indicator and microphone icon on a dark gradient background" height="738"><br><span>watchOS 27 beta 3 introduces a dedicated Siri app.</span></div><br>At <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/wwdc" title="WWDC" data-kpt="1">WWDC</a> 2026, Apple's virtual assistant <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/08/new-more-personal-siri-ai-is-set-to-arrive-in-2026">received</a> a long-overdue upgrade. <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/siri" title="Siri" data-kpt="1">Siri</a> AI, available on <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ios-27" title="iOS 27" data-kpt="1">iOS 27</a> beta, supports contextual awareness and understands natural language, effectively making it an Apple-designed chatbot.<br><br>Initially, Siri AI was made available to developers with an <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhone</a>, <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ipad" title="iPad" data-kpt="1">iPad</a>, <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/mac" title="Mac" data-kpt="1">Mac</a>, or <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-vision-pro" title="Apple Vision Pro" data-kpt="1">Apple Vision Pro</a>. With Monday's watchOS 27 developer beta, however, the revamped digital assistant has made its way to the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-watch" title="Apple Watch" data-kpt="1">Apple Watch</a>, in the form of a dedicated Siri app.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/watchos-27-beta-3-brings-siri-ai-to-the-apple-watch?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244885?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: Apple Watch owners can now finally use the new-and-improved Siri AI, thanks to <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/watchos-27" title="watchOS 27" data-kpt="1">watchOS 27</a> beta 3.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68173-143712-Siriwatch-Cropped-xl.jpg" alt="Apple Watch screen showing Siri request: Hey Siri, show me some good stretches for running recovery, with a searching status indicator and microphone icon on a dark gradient background" height="738"><br><span>watchOS 27 beta 3 introduces a dedicated Siri app.</span></div><br>At <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/wwdc" title="WWDC" data-kpt="1">WWDC</a> 2026, Apple's virtual assistant <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/08/new-more-personal-siri-ai-is-set-to-arrive-in-2026">received</a> a long-overdue upgrade. <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/siri" title="Siri" data-kpt="1">Siri</a> AI, available on <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ios-27" title="iOS 27" data-kpt="1">iOS 27</a> beta, supports contextual awareness and understands natural language, effectively making it an Apple-designed chatbot.<br><br>Initially, Siri AI was made available to developers with an <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhone</a>, <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ipad" title="iPad" data-kpt="1">iPad</a>, <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/mac" title="Mac" data-kpt="1">Mac</a>, or <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-vision-pro" title="Apple Vision Pro" data-kpt="1">Apple Vision Pro</a>. With Monday's watchOS 27 developer beta, however, the revamped digital assistant has made its way to the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-watch" title="Apple Watch" data-kpt="1">Apple Watch</a>, in the form of a dedicated Siri app.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/watchos-27-beta-3-brings-siri-ai-to-the-apple-watch?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244885?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: B&H is blowing out M4 Pro MacBook Pro inventory at $1,799
item.description: B&H is clearing out M4 Pro MacBook Pro inventory, offering discounts of up to $500 off and prices as low as $1,799. But inventory is limited, and the deals may sell out quickly.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68172-143711-macbook-pro-14-up-to-500-off-xl.jpg" alt="Open MacBook Pro laptop on colorful gradient background with bold white text across the screen reading SAVE UP TO $500, suggesting a promotional discount on the laptop" height="720"><br><span>Save up to $500 on blowout 14-inch MacBook Pro laptops - Image credit: Apple</span></div><br>Kicking off the sale is Apple's last-gen M4 Pro 14-inch laptop that's <strong><a href="https://www.bhphotovideo.com/c/product/1859564-REG/apple_mx2e3ll_a_14_macbook_pro_m4.html/BI/1717/KBID/2301/SID/da-macp-14in-m4-pro-1799-070626" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">marked down to $1,799</a></strong>. This configuration in Apple's silver finish has a 12-core CPU and 16-core GPU, along with 24GB of unified memory and 512GB of storage.<br><br><a href="https://www.bhphotovideo.com/c/product/1859564-REG/apple_mx2e3ll_a_14_macbook_pro_m4.html/BI/1717/KBID/2301/SID/da-macp-14in-m4-pro-1799-btn-070626" rel="nofollow" class="deal-highlight">Buy M4 Pro MacBook Pro for $1,799</a><br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/bh-is-blowing-out-m4-pro-macbook-pro-inventory-at-1799?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244884?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: B&H is clearing out M4 Pro MacBook Pro inventory, offering discounts of up to $500 off and prices as low as $1,799. But inventory is limited, and the deals may sell out quickly.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68172-143711-macbook-pro-14-up-to-500-off-xl.jpg" alt="Open MacBook Pro laptop on colorful gradient background with bold white text across the screen reading SAVE UP TO $500, suggesting a promotional discount on the laptop" height="720"><br><span>Save up to $500 on blowout 14-inch MacBook Pro laptops - Image credit: Apple</span></div><br>Kicking off the sale is Apple's last-gen M4 Pro 14-inch laptop that's <strong><a href="https://www.bhphotovideo.com/c/product/1859564-REG/apple_mx2e3ll_a_14_macbook_pro_m4.html/BI/1717/KBID/2301/SID/da-macp-14in-m4-pro-1799-070626" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">marked down to $1,799</a></strong>. This configuration in Apple's silver finish has a 12-core CPU and 16-core GPU, along with 24GB of unified memory and 512GB of storage.<br><br><a href="https://www.bhphotovideo.com/c/product/1859564-REG/apple_mx2e3ll_a_14_macbook_pro_m4.html/BI/1717/KBID/2301/SID/da-macp-14in-m4-pro-1799-btn-070626" rel="nofollow" class="deal-highlight">Buy M4 Pro MacBook Pro for $1,799</a><br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/bh-is-blowing-out-m4-pro-macbook-pro-inventory-at-1799?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244884?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: New in iOS 27 beta 3: Siri AI voice customization options
item.description: The third developer beta of <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ios-27" title="iOS 27" data-kpt="1">iOS 27</a> is here, with added customization options for Siri AI, visual tweaks, and more. Here's what's new.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68171-143710-27b3-Cropped-xl.jpg" alt="iPhone screen showing Software Update page with iOS 27 Beta 3 selected, 20.02 GB download, dark mode interface, and a gradient red background behind the phone" height="738"><br><span>iOS 27 beta 3 delivers new customization options for Siri AI.</span></div><br>On Monday, two weeks after the arrival of iOS 27 beta 2, Apple made <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/apple-rolls-out-the-third-ios-27-macos-27-developer-betas">the third</a> developer beta available for download. While iOS 27 beta 1 <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/08/ios-27-macos-27-developer-betas-are-out-but-you-should-probably-wait">delivered</a> the long-overdue <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/siri" title="Siri" data-kpt="1">Siri</a> AI, <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/22/new-in-ios-27-beta-2-update-an-apple-tv-in-the-home-app-wallet-insights">the second beta</a> included enhancements for the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-home" title="Apple Home" data-kpt="1">Apple Home</a> and <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-wallet" title="Apple Wallet" data-kpt="1">Apple Wallet</a> apps.<br><br>Notably, for most, the entire Siri AI model needs to be redownloaded between beta 2 and beta 3. For some users, this has made the features gated behind a wait-list again.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/new-in-ios-27-beta-3-siri-ai-voice-customization-options?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244883?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

While iOS 27 beta 1 <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/08/ios-27-macos-27-developer-betas-are-out-but-you-should-probably-wait">delivered</a> the long-overdue <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/siri" title="Siri" data-kpt="1">Siri</a> AI, <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/22/new-in-ios-27-beta-2-update-an-apple-tv-in-the-home-app-wallet-insights">the second beta</a> included enhancements for the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-home" title="Apple Home" data-kpt="1">Apple Home</a> and <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-wallet" title="Apple Wallet" data-kpt="1">Apple Wallet</a> apps.<br><br>Notably, for most, the entire Siri AI model needs to be redownloaded between beta 2 and beta 3. item.title: Apple rolls out the third iOS 27, macOS 27 developer betas
item.description: Apple has now moved to the third round for the developer betas of <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ios-27" title="iOS 27" data-kpt="1">iOS 27</a>, macOS 27, and others of the 27 generation. Expect more to come before the eventual fall releases.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68056-143709-67884-143086-ios27devbeta1-xl-xl.jpg" alt="Close-up of an iPhone screen on the Software Update page, showing iOS 17 beta update details with description text and a prominent blue Update Now button at the bottom" height="738"><br><span>An earlier iOS 27 beta screen</span></div><br>The developer beta program for the 27-gen operating systems is continuing with its third round of builds. All to make sure that the versions that ship in the fall are in top working order for the general public.<br><br>The third developer builds arrive after the second, which arrived on <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/22/second-developer-betas-of-ios-27-macos-27-are-out">June 22</a> for most of the operating systems. The <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/watchos-27" title="watchOS 27" data-kpt="1">watchOS 27</a> counterparts landed later, on <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/23/apple-rolls-out-watchos-27-beta-2-for-apple-watch">June 23</a> and <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/23/apple-rolls-out-watchos-27-beta-2-for-apple-watch">June 25. </a><br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/apple-rolls-out-the-third-ios-27-macos-27-developer-betas?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244881?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Fourth developer betas of iOS 26.6, macOS 26.6 land for testing
item.description: Apple's beta program for its 26-gen operating systems continues, with the fourth developer builds of <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ios-26" title="iOS 26" data-kpt="1">iOS 26.6</a> and others now available for testing.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68004-143370-63899-132994-wwdc_2025_____june_9___apple_8-22_screenshot-xl-xl.jpg" alt="Apple devices lineup including a MacBook laptop, iPad tablet, iPhone, Apple Watch, and a black VR headset, all displaying colorful app screens on a light background" height="720" class=""><br><span>Apple's hardware that works with the 26-generation operating systems - Image Credit: Apple</span></div><br>While the main attention is on the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/08/ios-27-macos-27-developer-betas-are-out-but-you-should-probably-wait">27-generation</a>, Apple is still making changes to its current-gen operating systems.<br><br>The fourth developer betas landed after the third, which landed on <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/29/third-developer-betas-of-ios-266-macos-266-arrive-with-ios-2652-release">June 29</a>. The second was distributed on <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/15/second-developer-betas-for-ios-266-macos-266-have-arrived">June 15</a>, and the first round came out on <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/05/26/first-betas-of-ios-266-macos-266-arrive-as-wwdcs-gen-27-draws-near">May 26</a>.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/fourth-developer-betas-of-ios-266-macos-266-land-for-testing?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244882?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

The second was distributed on <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/15/second-developer-betas-for-ios-266-macos-266-have-arrived">June 15</a>, and the first round came out on <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/05/26/first-betas-of-ios-266-macos-266-arrive-as-wwdcs-gen-27-draws-near">May 26</a>.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/fourth-developer-betas-of-ios-266-macos-266-land-for-testing?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244882?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: iPhone 17 Pro Max will represent America's present, in 250 years
item.description: An <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-17" title="iPhone 17" data-kpt="1">iPhone 17</a> Pro Max will represent American innovation in the nation's official time capsule, joining a collection that reflects how the United States chose to remember itself in 2026.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68170-143707-9BF9C4B8-9024-4134-B2D2-E94CE88EFE1B-xl.jpg" alt="Close-up of a smartphone screen showing a colorful gradient background and the America 250 logo, with time, battery, and signal icons visible at the top against a purple backdrop" height="738"><span>America 250</span></div><br>America250 has sealed the nation's official time capsule with an iPhone 17 Pro Max inside. The capsule preserves a snapshot of the United States that won't be reopened until 2276.<br><br>The collection will be buried on July 4 at Independence National Historical Park in Philadelphia as part of the country's 250th anniversary celebration. Organizers chose the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhone</a> to represent American innovation during the semiquincentennial.<br><br>America250 says the iPhone 17 Pro Max represents advances in handheld computing, photography, and connectivity. Few consumer products have changed how people communicate, work, and create as profoundly as Apple's smartphone.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/iphone-17-pro-max-will-represent-americas-present-in-250-years?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244880?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

Organizers chose the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhone</a> to represent American innovation during the semiquincentennial.<br><br>America250 says the iPhone 17 Pro Max represents advances in handheld computing, photography, and connectivity. item.title: Apple Watch Ultra 3 drops to $699 with $100 Amazon discount
item.description: Amazon has knocked $100 off the Apple Watch Ultra 3, dropping Apple's premium smartwatch to $699.99 for a limited time.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68168-143696-apple-watch-ultra-3-100-off-sale-xl.jpg" alt="Promotional graphic showing a black Apple Watch Ultra 3 with detailed watch face, bold text announcing BEST PRICE and Apple Watch Ultra 3 100 dollars off on dark geometric background" height="720"><br><span>Save $100 on the Apple Watch Ultra 3 at Amazon - Image credit: Apple</span></div><br>Amazon has cut the price of the Apple Watch Ultra 3 to <a href="https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FQFHVZYL?th=1&tag=apinsiderdeals-20" rel="nofollow" target="_blank"><strong>$699.99</strong></a>, delivering a solid $100 discount on Apple's premium smartwatch. While we've seen the wearable dip to as low as $649 this year, today's offer remains one of the best widely available deals on the current-generation model.<br><br><a href="https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0FQFHVZYL?th=1&tag=apinsiderdeals-20" rel="nofollow" class="deal-highlight">Buy Apple Watch Ultra 3 for $699.99</a><br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/apple-watch-ultra-3-drops-to-699-with-100-amazon-discount?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244879?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: iPhone 18 Pro Max may get significantly longer battery life after all
item.description: Despite previous reports saying the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-18" title="iPhone 18" data-kpt="1">iPhone 18 Pro</a> range will see only fractional improvements to battery life, a cautious leaker now claims that might not be the case with the iPhone 18 Pro Max.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/65718-137639-65285-136517-iphone_17_pro_max_camera_2-xl-xl.jpg" alt="Close-up of a blue smartphone on a dark surface, featuring three raised camera lenses and side buttons visible." height="738"><br><span>The current iPhone 17 Pro Max</span></div><br><table><br><br><tbody><tr><td>Model</td><td><strong>US</strong></td><td><strong>China</strong></td></tr><br><br><br> <strong>Rumor Score:</strong> 🤯 Likely <br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/iphone-18-pro-max-may-get-significantly-longer-battery-life-after-all?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244878?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Apple & Broadcom renew partnership through 2031
item.description: Apple has extended its long-time supplier agreement with Broadcom, ensuring Apple gets a steady flow of custom chips until 2031.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68167-143695-61601-127281-000-lede-Broadcom-xl-xl.jpg" alt="Broadcom corporate sign with red logo in front of a modern glass office building, under a blue sky with some clouds and a United States flag in the background" height="731"><br><span>Broadcom offices - Image Credit: Broadcom</span></div><br>Apple has used Broadcom as a supplier for its <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhone</a> and other hardware for quite some time. However, as threats like the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/25/apple-confirms-price-nikes-across-macs-ipads-and-more">global memory crisis</a> demonstrate, supply chains often aren't as stable as they could be.<br><br>On July 6, Broadcom <a href="https://www.reuters.com/technology/broadcom-apple-extend-chip-partnership-through-2031-2026-07-06/">confirmed</a> to <em>Reuters</em> that it had agreed to an extension of its partnership with Apple. The deal means Broadcom will be developing and supplying custom chips for Apple through to 2031.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/apple-broadcom-renew-partnership-through-2031?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244877?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: Apple has extended its long-time supplier agreement with Broadcom, ensuring Apple gets a steady flow of custom chips until 2031.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68167-143695-61601-127281-000-lede-Broadcom-xl-xl.jpg" alt="Broadcom corporate sign with red logo in front of a modern glass office building, under a blue sky with some clouds and a United States flag in the background" height="731"><br><span>Broadcom offices - Image Credit: Broadcom</span></div><br>Apple has used Broadcom as a supplier for its <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhone</a> and other hardware for quite some time. However, as threats like the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/25/apple-confirms-price-nikes-across-macs-ipads-and-more">global memory crisis</a> demonstrate, supply chains often aren't as stable as they could be.<br><br>On July 6, Broadcom <a href="https://www.reuters.com/technology/broadcom-apple-extend-chip-partnership-through-2031-2026-07-06/">confirmed</a> to <em>Reuters</em> that it had agreed to an extension of its partnership with Apple. The deal means Broadcom will be developing and supplying custom chips for Apple through to 2031.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/apple-broadcom-renew-partnership-through-2031?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244877?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Four years later, Apple bends to India's demands over card payments
item.description: <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/app-store" title="App Store" data-kpt="1">App Store</a> and iCloud users in India should soon be able to subscribe using credit or debit cards again, as Apple tries to smooth the way to launching Apple Pay.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/54016-108739-000-lead-Apple-BKC-opening-xl.jpg" alt="Dierdre O'Brien and Tim Cook at Apple BKC (Source: Apple)" height="720"><br><span>Dierdre O'Brien and Tim Cook open Apple BKC, the first Apple Store in India (Source: Apple)</span></div><br>Starting in June 2022, India introduced extra banking laws regarding any online recurring payments, such as subscriptions, and Apple <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/22/05/20/apple-confirms-it-wont-accept-card-payments-in-india-cites-banking-laws">ceased accepting</a> any debit or credit cards in the country. The company said at the time only that it was because of banking regulations and that users would have to switch to India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI).<br><br>Now according to Indian financial publication <em>Moneycontrol</em>, Apple <a href="https://www.moneycontrol.com/technology/apple-restarts-card-payments-for-app-store-and-icloud-in-india-sources-article-13966811.html">is testing</a> credit and debit card options with a limited number of users. Reportedly, the intention is to roll it out across India in the next few months.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/four-years-on-apple-bends-to-indias-demands-over-card-payments?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244876?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/app-store" title="App Store" data-kpt="1">App Store</a> and iCloud users in India should soon be able to subscribe using credit or debit cards again, as Apple tries to smooth the way to launching Apple Pay.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/54016-108739-000-lead-Apple-BKC-opening-xl.jpg" alt="Dierdre O'Brien and Tim Cook at Apple BKC (Source: Apple)" height="720"><br><span>Dierdre O'Brien and Tim Cook open Apple BKC, the first Apple Store in India (Source: Apple)</span></div><br>Starting in June 2022, India introduced extra banking laws regarding any online recurring payments, such as subscriptions, and Apple <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/22/05/20/apple-confirms-it-wont-accept-card-payments-in-india-cites-banking-laws">ceased accepting</a> any debit or credit cards in the country. The company said at the time only that it was because of banking regulations and that users would have to switch to India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI).<br><br>Now according to Indian financial publication <em>Moneycontrol</em>, Apple <a href="https://www.moneycontrol.com/technology/apple-restarts-card-payments-for-app-store-and-icloud-in-india-sources-article-13966811.html">is testing</a> credit and debit card options with a limited number of users. Reportedly, the intention is to roll it out across India in the next few months.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/06/four-years-on-apple-bends-to-indias-demands-over-card-payments?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244876?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Plex's $249.99 five-year pass arrives after $749.99 Lifetime Pass price hike
item.description: Shortly after increasing the lifetime version of the Plex Plan to $749.99, Plex has now introduced a new subscription, charging $249.99 for five years of streaming media from your <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/mac" title="Mac" data-kpt="1">Mac</a> to your iPhone.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68160-143673-68093-143536-67688-142663-49179-96017-plex-header-xl-xl-xl-xl.jpg" alt="Plex logo with a bold yellow arrow inside a dark circle on a blurred background of various movie and TV show posters" height="731"><br><span>Plex logo</span></div><br>On <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/06/27/psa-lifetime-plex-plan-goes-from-24999-to-a-painful-74999-on-july-1">July 1</a>, Plex significantly increased the cost of its Lifetime Plex Plan from $249.99 to a <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/05/19/ludicrous-plex-lifetime-pass-increase-to-74999-is-too-expensive-for-watching-your-owned-media">staggering $749.99</a>. While consumers will have missed the boat to get the old pricing, they can still <a href="https://www.dpbolvw.net/click-1989299-15734677?sid=news-plex-price-change-062726-xid-fr1783178861661egh" rel="nofollow">spend $249.99</a> on something that's not quite as everlasting.<br><br>Subscribers can sign up for a new five-year Plex Pass. The new option, offered alongside the monthly $6.99 and annual $69.99 subscriptions, gives users a lengthy period of usage without paying a regular subscription.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/04/plexs-24999-five-year-pass-arrives-after-74999-lifetime-pass-price-hike?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244872?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Siri AI is finally good, and Apple is going to win the AI wars
item.description: Apple is doing it again, it is coming late to the party. But it will eventually dominate AI because of how it thinks about users and about use cases where rivals consider only technical issues.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68145-143632-000-lead-Siri-AI-Wrong-xl.jpg" alt="Close-up of a smartphone screen showing a dark notification banner at the top with the error message: Something went wrong. Please try again, above a blue lock screen clock reading 11:34" height="720"><br><span>Hopefully this is just a beta issue, but this Siri AI error comes up a lot.</span></div><br>Even when testing the betas of <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/siri" title="Siri" data-kpt="1">Siri AI</a> on the same or similar devices, everyone at <em>AppleInsider</em> is having different experiences. For instance, I found that in the first developer beta on both the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/mac" title="Mac" data-kpt="1">Mac</a> and the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhone</a>, Siri AI could be staggeringly irritating and sometimes no better than the old Siri. With the third developer beta of <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/macos-golden-gate" title="macOS Golden Gate" data-kpt="1">macOS Golden Gate</a>, Siri AI would sometimes just abandon any request I make of it, but was always fine for everyone else.<br><br>Across all of the betas, though, we are all finding that there are things Siri AI can do that are exceptional, and better than its rivals. Those irritations will surely be fixed before the public release, too.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/siri-ai-is-finally-good-and-apple-is-going-to-with-the-ai-wars?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244867?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: Apple is doing it again, it is coming late to the party. But it will eventually dominate AI because of how it thinks about users and about use cases where rivals consider only technical issues.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68145-143632-000-lead-Siri-AI-Wrong-xl.jpg" alt="Close-up of a smartphone screen showing a dark notification banner at the top with the error message: Something went wrong. Please try again, above a blue lock screen clock reading 11:34" height="720"><br><span>Hopefully this is just a beta issue, but this Siri AI error comes up a lot.</span></div><br>Even when testing the betas of <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/siri" title="Siri" data-kpt="1">Siri AI</a> on the same or similar devices, everyone at <em>AppleInsider</em> is having different experiences. For instance, I found that in the first developer beta on both the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/mac" title="Mac" data-kpt="1">Mac</a> and the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhone</a>, Siri AI could be staggeringly irritating and sometimes no better than the old Siri. With the third developer beta of <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/macos-golden-gate" title="macOS Golden Gate" data-kpt="1">macOS Golden Gate</a>, Siri AI would sometimes just abandon any request I make of it, but was always fine for everyone else.<br><br>Across all of the betas, though, we are all finding that there are things Siri AI can do that are exceptional, and better than its rivals. Those irritations will surely be fixed before the public release, too.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/siri-ai-is-finally-good-and-apple-is-going-to-with-the-ai-wars?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244867?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Sunday Reboot: Stats, iPhones, and AI devices are still a threat
item.description: In this week's "Sunday Reboot," scientists somehow say <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhones</a> lowered birthrates, and Musk's AI prototype story shows that specialist hardware is coming, even if we don't necessarily want it.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68161-143677-MuskHumanepin-xl.jpg" alt="Man in a dark blazer on stage thoughtfully touching his chin, standing beside a large graphic of a sleek white gadget with a black and yellow camera module" height="738"><br><span>Elon Musk denies there's an xAI AI device being prototyped - Image Credit: Tesla</span></div><br><em>Sunday Reboot</em> is a weekly column covering some of the lighter stories within the Apple reality distortion field from the past seven days. All to get the next week underway with a good first step.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/05/sunday-reboot-stats-iphones-and-ai-devices-are-still-a-threat?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244874?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Folding iPhone may launch in September, good luck getting it before 2027
item.description: Apple may be pulling an iPhone X for the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-fold" title="iPhone Fold" data-kpt="1">iPhone Fold</a>, by launching it in September but actually selling it months later.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68162-143678-Squat-Fold-9-xl.jpg" alt="Foldable smartphone standing partly open on a wooden desk, displaying colorful wavy lines; in the background, a potted succulent, a glowing cat-shaped light, and a closed laptop" height="738"><br><span>What the iPhone Fold could look like - Image Credit: AppleInsider</span></div><br>Apple is widely expected to launch the iPhone Fold this fall, alongside the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-18" title="iPhone 18" data-kpt="1">iPhone 18</a> Pro. However, the schedule of when it goes on sale could be completely different.<br><br>According to a Sunday X <a href="https://x.com/mingchikuo/status/2073770705905123705">post</a> by TF Securities analyst <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ming-chi-kuo" title="Ming-Chi Kuo" data-kpt="1">Ming-Chi Kuo</a>, the supply chain indicates there may not be enough inventory for the iPhone Fold to go on sale immediately after launch.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/05/folding-iphone-may-launch-in-september-good-luck-getting-it-before-2027?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244873?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: Apple may be pulling an iPhone X for the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-fold" title="iPhone Fold" data-kpt="1">iPhone Fold</a>, by launching it in September but actually selling it months later.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68162-143678-Squat-Fold-9-xl.jpg" alt="Foldable smartphone standing partly open on a wooden desk, displaying colorful wavy lines; in the background, a potted succulent, a glowing cat-shaped light, and a closed laptop" height="738"><br><span>What the iPhone Fold could look like - Image Credit: AppleInsider</span></div><br>Apple is widely expected to launch the iPhone Fold this fall, alongside the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-18" title="iPhone 18" data-kpt="1">iPhone 18</a> Pro. However, the schedule of when it goes on sale could be completely different.<br><br>According to a Sunday X <a href="https://x.com/mingchikuo/status/2073770705905123705">post</a> by TF Securities analyst <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ming-chi-kuo" title="Ming-Chi Kuo" data-kpt="1">Ming-Chi Kuo</a>, the supply chain indicates there may not be enough inventory for the iPhone Fold to go on sale immediately after launch.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/05/folding-iphone-may-launch-in-september-good-luck-getting-it-before-2027?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244873?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Three changes Apple could do to make iPhone Air 2 a hit
item.description: With <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-air" title="iPhone Air" data-kpt="1">iPhone Air</a> 2 rumored to be less than a year away, Apple could turn to better cooling or more cameras to make the ultra-thin phone even more popular than the original. Here's what's been rumored, and some other possibilities.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68131-143659-Holding-iPhone-Air-xl.jpg" alt="Hand holding a white smartphone with a single rear camera, viewed from the back, over a gray fabric surface, with another similar phone lying flat in the background" height="738"><br><span>iPhone Air is a great phone, but the second-generation could be even better</span></div><br>Despite what some vocal online detractors say, iPhone Air has its fans. I'm certainly counted among them.<br><br><a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/25/10/22/relax—-apple-tweaking-production-numbers-on-new-iphone-models-is-normal">Early reports</a> said Apple's thinnest phone was a "failure" and that the second-generation was cancelled. Now that we're three-quarters of the way through the iPhone Air first-year release cycle, it's more evident that the phone isn't a "failure" and a second-generation model is coming.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/three-changes-apple-could-do-to-make-iphone-air-2-a-hit?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244871?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: With <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-air" title="iPhone Air" data-kpt="1">iPhone Air</a> 2 rumored to be less than a year away, Apple could turn to better cooling or more cameras to make the ultra-thin phone even more popular than the original. Here's what's been rumored, and some other possibilities.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68131-143659-Holding-iPhone-Air-xl.jpg" alt="Hand holding a white smartphone with a single rear camera, viewed from the back, over a gray fabric surface, with another similar phone lying flat in the background" height="738"><br><span>iPhone Air is a great phone, but the second-generation could be even better</span></div><br>Despite what some vocal online detractors say, iPhone Air has its fans. I'm certainly counted among them.<br><br><a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/25/10/22/relax—-apple-tweaking-production-numbers-on-new-iphone-models-is-normal">Early reports</a> said Apple's thinnest phone was a "failure" and that the second-generation was cancelled. Now that we're three-quarters of the way through the iPhone Air first-year release cycle, it's more evident that the phone isn't a "failure" and a second-generation model is coming.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/three-changes-apple-could-do-to-make-iphone-air-2-a-hit?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244871?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Leaker Jon Prosser denies Apple's charges, rolls co-defendant under the bus
item.description: Jon Prosser has formally responded to Apple's lawsuit over alleged <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ios" title="iOS" data-kpt="1">iOS</a> leaks, claiming that he is entirely innocent and that colleague Michael Ramacciotti is solely responsible for any alleged trade secret theft.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/64405-134198-000-lede-Prosser-xl.jpg" alt="A man in a black jacket is gesturing while standing in a modern glass corridor with trees visible outside." height="720"><br><span>Jon Prosser in a still from one of his Front Page Tech videos — image credit: Prosser</span></div><br>Apple's now <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/25/07/18/apple-sues-jon-prosser-over-ios-26-leaks">year-long case</a> against Prosser had initially seen the YouTuber repeatedly <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/25/10/20/apples-leaker-lawsuit-marches-on-after-jon-prosser-misses-deadlines">ignoring court deadlines</a>. He began responding in <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/04/14/jon-prossers-silence-stalls-apples-leak-lawsuit-as-discovery-drags-on">April 2026</a>, and his lawyers have now formally filed his rebuttal to Apple's accusations that he and Ramacciotti conspired to steal trade secrets from Apple employee Ethan Lipnik.<br><br>"Ramacciotti's is [sic] responsible for all harm caused to Prosser and should indemnify him for all harm caused," says the filing. "Ramacciotti's act of displaying the features was not induced by Prosser and, as such, Ramacciotti is completely responsible for the disclosure of Apple's alleged trade secrets, if any."<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/leaker-jon-prosser-denies-apples-charges-and-blames-everything-on-his-co-defendant?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244863?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: Jon Prosser has formally responded to Apple's lawsuit over alleged <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ios" title="iOS" data-kpt="1">iOS</a> leaks, claiming that he is entirely innocent and that colleague Michael Ramacciotti is solely responsible for any alleged trade secret theft.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/64405-134198-000-lede-Prosser-xl.jpg" alt="A man in a black jacket is gesturing while standing in a modern glass corridor with trees visible outside." height="720"><br><span>Jon Prosser in a still from one of his Front Page Tech videos — image credit: Prosser</span></div><br>Apple's now <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/25/07/18/apple-sues-jon-prosser-over-ios-26-leaks">year-long case</a> against Prosser had initially seen the YouTuber repeatedly <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/25/10/20/apples-leaker-lawsuit-marches-on-after-jon-prosser-misses-deadlines">ignoring court deadlines</a>. He began responding in <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/04/14/jon-prossers-silence-stalls-apples-leak-lawsuit-as-discovery-drags-on">April 2026</a>, and his lawyers have now formally filed his rebuttal to Apple's accusations that he and Ramacciotti conspired to steal trade secrets from Apple employee Ethan Lipnik.<br><br>"Ramacciotti's is [sic] responsible for all harm caused to Prosser and should indemnify him for all harm caused," says the filing. "Ramacciotti's act of displaying the features was not induced by Prosser and, as such, Ramacciotti is completely responsible for the disclosure of Apple's alleged trade secrets, if any."<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/leaker-jon-prosser-denies-apples-charges-and-blames-everything-on-his-co-defendant?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244863?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: AirPods with cameras show up in iOS 27 beta code
item.description: Code in the latest iOS 27 developer beta describes handling images from a pair of cameras, and hints that these are surely the expected <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/airpods" title="AirPods" data-kpt="1">AirPods</a> with cameras.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68156-143664-000-lead-AirPods-Pro-d2-xl.jpg" alt="Close-up of white wireless earbuds resting in an open charging case, highlighting the smooth plastic surface and small black speaker grilles" height="720" class=""><br><span>The current AirPods Pro in their charging case.</span></div><br>AirPods with cameras may have <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/24/06/30/new-airpods-with-ir-camera-coming-to-enhance-spatial-audio-in-apple-vision-pro">long been expected</a>, but the most recent leak claimed that Apple <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/airpods-with-cameras-wont-be-coming-any-time-soon-after-all">has "suspended"</a> the whole project. Whether it has or not, the device got far enough that there are references to it in the latest <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/ios-27" title="iOS 27" data-kpt="1">iOS 27</a> developer beta.<br><br><div><blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">/System/Library/AssetsV2/com_apple_MobileAsset_UAF_IF_PlannerOverrides/purpose_auto/9aaa6a204118137235983cc3f1eecae8a125c550.asset/AssetData/PCC/system_prompt_metadata/system_prompt.json seems to hint at some smart glasses codenamed B790 <a href="https://t.co/IEmbfleth4">pic.twitter.com/IEmbfleth4</a></p>— sam henri gold (@samhenrigold) <a href="https://x.com/samhenrigold/status/2073011675880444246?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">July 3, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.x.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script></div><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/airpods-with-cameras-show-up-in-ios-27-beta-code?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244870?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: iPhone 18 Pro leaks, AirDrop security concerns, and increased iPhone Fold orders, on the AppleInsider Podcast
item.description: New leaks about the iPhone 18 Pro seem to be annoying Apple, while the guessing game continues over whether the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-fold" title="iPhone Fold" data-kpt="1">iPhone Fold</a> is coming in September, plus why we should not believe that the iPhone is the end of civilization, on the AppleInsider Podcast.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68154-143657-645-lede-Podcast-article-xl.jpg" alt="Close-up of a sleek smartphone corner showing two large rear camera lenses and a flash, with a small circular ai logo in the upper left corner" height="720"><br><span>Mockup of how the cameras may look on the expected iPhone Fold - image credit: AppleInsider</span></div><br>Among the more than 630GB of data taken from a Tata <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhone</a> plant in India this week, were documents concerning plans for the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-18" title="iPhone 18" data-kpt="1">iPhone 18 Pro</a>. They included how the US is currently expected to continue using Qualcomm modems in this next iPhone, while the rest of world will get Apple's own C2 one.<br><br>Then there are also a few but tantalizing details of camera updates, which hint at the possibility of a variable aperture system finally coming to the iPhone.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/iphone-18-pro-leaks-airdrop-security-concerns-and-increased-iphone-fold-orders-on-the-appleinsider-podcast?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244869?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: New leaks about the iPhone 18 Pro seem to be annoying Apple, while the guessing game continues over whether the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-fold" title="iPhone Fold" data-kpt="1">iPhone Fold</a> is coming in September, plus why we should not believe that the iPhone is the end of civilization, on the AppleInsider Podcast.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68154-143657-645-lede-Podcast-article-xl.jpg" alt="Close-up of a sleek smartphone corner showing two large rear camera lenses and a flash, with a small circular ai logo in the upper left corner" height="720"><br><span>Mockup of how the cameras may look on the expected iPhone Fold - image credit: AppleInsider</span></div><br>Among the more than 630GB of data taken from a Tata <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone" title="iPhone" data-kpt="1">iPhone</a> plant in India this week, were documents concerning plans for the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/iphone-18" title="iPhone 18" data-kpt="1">iPhone 18 Pro</a>. They included how the US is currently expected to continue using Qualcomm modems in this next iPhone, while the rest of world will get Apple's own C2 one.<br><br>Then there are also a few but tantalizing details of camera updates, which hint at the possibility of a variable aperture system finally coming to the iPhone.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/iphone-18-pro-leaks-airdrop-security-concerns-and-increased-iphone-fold-orders-on-the-appleinsider-podcast?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244869?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Apple Watch Series 12 may get new health sensor in its band
item.description: A leaker with a reasonable track record, except regarding the <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/apple-watch" title="Apple Watch" data-kpt="1">Apple Watch</a>, claims that the Apple Watch Series 12 will feature a new health sensor, but only in its fluoroelastomer band.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/62408-129353-000-lede-Apple-Watch-Sport-Band-xl.jpg" alt="Smartwatch with large white face, bold black numbers, and a silver case on a dark blue band." height="738"><br><span>Apple Watch with the basic band - image credit: Apple</span></div><br>Apple Watch <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/24/12/03/apple-watch-tracks-more-health-metrics-than-you-realize">already tracks</a> a huge number of different health metrics, but Apple has regularly been rumored to add <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/23/04/06/future-apple-watch-band-could-detect-the-smallest-finger-movements-and-gestures">even more sensors</a> via a watch band. According to leaker Kosutami, the company is finally going to do it, although with one significant catch.<br><br><div><blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">[Censo-Wat]ch Series 12 gonna have a sensor on band, injection molded(to the silicon band, they hasn't yet figured out how to putting a sensor on band with other materials now.).</p>— Kosutami (@Kosutami_Ito) <a href="https://x.com/Kosutami_Ito/status/2072891680756552134?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">July 3, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.x.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script></div><br><br> <strong>Rumor Score:</strong> 🤔 Possible <br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/apple-watch-series-12-may-get-new-health-sensor-in-its-band?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244866?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

According to leaker Kosutami, the company is finally going to do it, although with one significant catch.<br><br><div><blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">[Censo-Wat]ch Series 12 gonna have a sensor on band, injection molded(to the silicon band, they hasn't yet figured out how to putting a sensor on band with other materials now.).</p>— Kosutami (@Kosutami_Ito) <a href="https://x.com/Kosutami_Ito/status/2072891680756552134?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">July 3, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.x.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script></div><br><br> <strong>Rumor Score:</strong> 🤔 Possible <br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/apple-watch-series-12-may-get-new-health-sensor-in-its-band?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244866?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: AirPods with Cameras won't be coming any time soon after all
item.description: A leaker with a fair but short track record, claims that Apple has suspended work on the expected <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/airpods" title="AirPods" data-kpt="1">AirPods</a> with cameras.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/66648-139760-AirPods-Pro-3-earbuds-xl.jpg" alt="Two white wireless earbuds with silicone tips and black accents lying on a soft gray fabric surface, shown in close-up with shallow depth of field"><br><span>Apple's current AirPods Pro</span></div><br>Back in <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/02/09/airpods-pro-with-built-in-cameras-may-not-cost-more-than-current-earbuds">February 2026</a>, leaker Kosutami claimed that AirPods with cameras were coming, and that they would stay the same price as current models. Now the same leaker is back saying that Apple has suspended the whole idea.<br><br>That's literally all this routinely brief leaker says. Reposting a previous claim about the device from June 2026 that said just "case concluded," Kosutami has now responded with the single word "Suspended."<br><br><br> <strong>Rumor Score:</strong> 🤔 Possible <br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/airpods-with-cameras-wont-be-coming-any-time-soon-after-all?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244864?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: A leaker with a fair but short track record, claims that Apple has suspended work on the expected <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/airpods" title="AirPods" data-kpt="1">AirPods</a> with cameras.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/66648-139760-AirPods-Pro-3-earbuds-xl.jpg" alt="Two white wireless earbuds with silicone tips and black accents lying on a soft gray fabric surface, shown in close-up with shallow depth of field"><br><span>Apple's current AirPods Pro</span></div><br>Back in <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/02/09/airpods-pro-with-built-in-cameras-may-not-cost-more-than-current-earbuds">February 2026</a>, leaker Kosutami claimed that AirPods with cameras were coming, and that they would stay the same price as current models. Now the same leaker is back saying that Apple has suspended the whole idea.<br><br>That's literally all this routinely brief leaker says. Reposting a previous claim about the device from June 2026 that said just "case concluded," Kosutami has now responded with the single word "Suspended."<br><br><br> <strong>Rumor Score:</strong> 🤔 Possible <br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/airpods-with-cameras-wont-be-coming-any-time-soon-after-all?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244864?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Grab OWC Thunderbolt docks, cables, enclosures from just $19.99 with exclusive deals
item.description: Exclusive 4th of July deals are in effect at OWC, dropping prices for enclosures, docks, and Thunderbolt 5 cables to as low as $19.99.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68150-143656-owc-dock-cable-enclosure-deals-xl.jpg" alt="Computer accessories including a docking station, external drive, cables, and a black desktop enclosure on a colorful starry background, with a highlighted banner reading DEALS FROM $19.99" height="720"><br><span>Save on OWC Thunderbolt 5 docks, cables, enclosures - Image credit: OWC</span></div><br>The sale includes a variety of accessories for your Mac or iPad, with OWC's <a href="https://otherworldcomputing.pxf.io/c/81003/3958934/50228?subId1=da-acc-owc-tb-dock-070326&u=https%3A%2F%2Feshop.macsales.com%2Fitem%2FOWC%2FTB5DOCK11P%2F" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">11-Port Thunderbolt 5 Dock</a> especially enticing at $229.99 after a $69 discount.<br><br>You can also pick up a Thunderbolt 5 cable <a href="https://otherworldcomputing.pxf.io/c/81003/3958934/50228?subId1=da-acc-owc-tb-dock-070326&u=https%3A%2F%2Feshop.macsales.com%2Fitem%2FOWC%2FCBLTB5C1.0M%2F" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">for $19.99</a> and a <a href="https://otherworldcomputing.pxf.io/c/81003/3958934/50228?subId1=da-acc-owc-tb-dock-070326&u=https%3A%2F%2Feshop.macsales.com%2Fitem%2FOWC%2FTB3IVKIT000%2F" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">ThunderBay 4 Enclosure</a> for $309.99. The four-bay Thunderbolt (40Gb/s) external storage enclosure for 2.5-inch and 3.5-inch SATA drives is a great way to expand your storage, especially if you're a content creator needing an abundance of space.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/grab-owc-thunderbolt-docks-cables-enclosures-from-just-1999-with-exclusive-deals?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244862?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Apple says public YouTube videos can be used in AI lawsuit defense
item.description: Apple is asking a federal court to dismiss the YouTuber AI training lawsuit, on the grounds that publicly available YouTube videos are lawfully accessible under both the DMCA and YouTube's Terms of Service.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68149-143655-AI-glass-xl.jpg" alt="Glowing silver Apple logo centered on a black background, surrounded by a neon multicolored looping outline forming a stylized star or atom shape" height="738"><br><span>Apple seeks dismissal of YouTube AI training lawsuit</span></div><br>In April 2026, a collection of YouTube channels <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/04/06/apple-may-have-scraped-youtube-videos-without-permission-for-ai-training">sued Apple</a>, claiming the company had scraped videos from YouTube to train internal AI models.<br><br>The class-action lawsuit was headed up by Ted Entertainment, owners of the h3h3Productions channels and podcast. Two golf channels, MrShortGameGolf and Golfholics, were also involved.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/apple-says-public-youtube-videos-can-be-used-in-ai-lawsuit-defense?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244861?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: Apple is asking a federal court to dismiss the YouTuber AI training lawsuit, on the grounds that publicly available YouTube videos are lawfully accessible under both the DMCA and YouTube's Terms of Service.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68149-143655-AI-glass-xl.jpg" alt="Glowing silver Apple logo centered on a black background, surrounded by a neon multicolored looping outline forming a stylized star or atom shape" height="738"><br><span>Apple seeks dismissal of YouTube AI training lawsuit</span></div><br>In April 2026, a collection of YouTube channels <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/04/06/apple-may-have-scraped-youtube-videos-without-permission-for-ai-training">sued Apple</a>, claiming the company had scraped videos from YouTube to train internal AI models.<br><br>The class-action lawsuit was headed up by Ted Entertainment, owners of the h3h3Productions channels and podcast. Two golf channels, MrShortGameGolf and Golfholics, were also involved.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/apple-says-public-youtube-videos-can-be-used-in-ai-lawsuit-defense?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244861?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: Anker Solix E10 review: Whole home battery backups finally come within reach
item.description: The Anker Solix E10 whole-home battery backup system is a modular setup that can keep your home powered and save you money with multiple forms of power input.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68130-143639-Anker-Solix-E10-and-Power-Dock-xl.jpg" alt="Compact wall-mounted power station with Anker logo connected by a charging cable to an adjacent control box, featuring several illuminated buttons in a dimly lit indoor setting" height="738"><br><span>Anker Solix E10 review: The E10 Power Module connected to the Power Dock.</span></div><br>In rural Ohio, my partner and I have struggled with power outages since we bought our house. The first weekend we moved in, we were hit with a power outage, and as we walked outside, we heard all of our neighbors turning on loud, gas-powered generators.<br><br>We knew this was common and had to find a solution. Until <a href="https://ankersolix.pxf.io/c/81003/2241990/29130?subId1=rv-othr-anker-solix-e10&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ankersolix.com%2Fproducts%2Fe10%3Fo_c%3D%26s_main%3DB17E1141%26varId%3D53737897853258" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">the Solix E10</a>, we've relied primarily on multiple battery backups in the house to keep the internet running, devices charged, and appliances going during these outages.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/anker-solix-e10-review-whole-home-battery-backups-finally-come-within-reach?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244860?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
item.content:encoded: The Anker Solix E10 whole-home battery backup system is a modular setup that can keep your home powered and save you money with multiple forms of power input.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68130-143639-Anker-Solix-E10-and-Power-Dock-xl.jpg" alt="Compact wall-mounted power station with Anker logo connected by a charging cable to an adjacent control box, featuring several illuminated buttons in a dimly lit indoor setting" height="738"><br><span>Anker Solix E10 review: The E10 Power Module connected to the Power Dock.</span></div><br>In rural Ohio, my partner and I have struggled with power outages since we bought our house. The first weekend we moved in, we were hit with a power outage, and as we walked outside, we heard all of our neighbors turning on loud, gas-powered generators.<br><br>We knew this was common and had to find a solution. Until <a href="https://ankersolix.pxf.io/c/81003/2241990/29130?subId1=rv-othr-anker-solix-e10&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ankersolix.com%2Fproducts%2Fe10%3Fo_c%3D%26s_main%3DB17E1141%26varId%3D53737897853258" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">the Solix E10</a>, we've relied primarily on multiple battery backups in the house to keep the internet running, devices charged, and appliances going during these outages.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/03/anker-solix-e10-review-whole-home-battery-backups-finally-come-within-reach?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244860?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>

item.title: What to expect from Apple's Q3 2026 results on July 30
item.description: Apple has confirmed that its financials for the third quarter of 2026 will be reported on July 30. It certainly will be an event with a lot of talking points from the quarter, and it might be the last one with Tim Cook in attendance.<br><br><div><img src="https://photos5.appleinsider.com/gallery/68132-143608-67173-141259-cook-financial-2022-xl-xl.jpg" alt="Smiling man in glasses and dark shirt waves with one hand, standing behind tall blue financial bar charts, with faint US dollar bills visible in the gray background" height="731"><br><span>The Q3 results will be Cook's last as CEO</span></div><br>Every three months, Apple issues a quarterly report revealing how well it's performed during the period. The third quarter results arrive at the end of July.<br><br>In a notification to investors and analysts, Apple states that it will be bringing out the third quarter results on July 30. The results will be followed by Apple's usual conference call at 5 p.m. EDT, featuring both current CEO <a href="https://appleinsider.com/inside/tim-cook" title="Tim Cook" data-kpt="1">Tim Cook</a> and CFO Kevan Parekh to talk about the numbers.<br><br><br> <a href="https://appleinsider.com/articles/26/07/02/what-to-expect-from-apples-q3-2026-results-on-july-30?utm_source=rss">Continue Reading on AppleInsider</a> | <a href="https://forums.appleinsider.com/discussion/244858?urm_source=rss">Discuss on our Forums</a>
